perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure of the Confid ential Information to any other person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to take commercially reasonable steps to prevent disclosure of the Confid ential Information to any other person or entity. For the avoidance of doubt, commercially reasonable steps do not include encrypting email communications.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 'reasonable steps' to prevent disclosure. The modification changes this to 'commercially reasonable steps' and explicitly excludes encrypting email communications. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 3, because Section 3 requires to 'protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) it uses to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and co nfidential information', meaning that if the company uses encryption on email communications to protect their information, it must do it on Confidential Information, creating this contradiction. If a party doesn't encrypt emails, they are not breaching this modified term. However, if the original text is upheld, they may be in breach if encryption is deemed a 'reasonable step'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information by using the same degree of care (but not less than a reasonable degree of care) it uses to protect the confidentiality of its own proprietary and co nfidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'reasonable steps' to 'commercially reasonable steps' and explicitly excludes email encryption. The contradicted text requires the same degree of care as the party uses for its own confidential information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care. If the party encrypts its own email communications, it's now unclear if the changed text allows them to avoid encrypting the Confidential Information and still be in compliance, creating a strong contradiction based on the ambiguity introduced. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, where key terms are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material, unless the recipient is required by law to retain such copies. In such cases, the retention must be only to what the law says and nothing more.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the recipient party to destroy all copies of Confidential Information. The modified text adds an exception for legal requirements, but this creates an in-text contradiction with the main provision that says it MUST destroy everything, which is now broken. This changes the core obligation to return and destroy all materials, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of written notice requesting return of any Confidential Information, the recipient Party will promptly (a) deliver to the disclosing Pa rty all Confidential Information furnished by the disclosing Party to the recipient Party, together with copies thereof, and (b) destroy materials generated by the recipient Party that include or re late to any part of the Confidential Information (including notes, analyses and compilations) without retaining a copy of any such material, unless the recipient is required by law to retain such copies. In such cases, the retention must be only to what the law says and nothing more.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the recipient to destroy all copies of Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception for legal requirements. This directly conflicts with the initial, absolute requirement to destroy everything, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the two clauses are now logically incompatible. The introduction of the exception changes the core obligation, leading to uncertainty in enforcement, directly adhering to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text in this case.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations of this Agreem ent shall not apply to information which (a) has entered the public domain except where such entry is the result of a Party’s breach of this Agreement or another agreement(s), (b) prior to disclosure hereunder was already rightfully in the receiving Party’s possession under no obligation of confidentiality , or (c) subsequent to disclosure hereunder is obtained by the receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a third party who has the right to disclose such information to the receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations of this Agreem ent shall not apply to information which (a) has entered the public domain , (b) prior to disclosure hereunder was already rightfully in the receiving Party’s possession under no obligation of confidentiality , or (c) subsequent to disclosure hereunder is obtained by the receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a third party who has the right to disclose such information to the receiving Party. However, Confidential Information disclosed to a Party’s consultant or agent, who then causes it to enter the public domain, is not exempt from confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text exempts information entering the public domain, except if caused by a breach. The modified text removes this exception for ANY breaches by the parties. This is then directly contradicted in the last added phrase where it specifies that if the Consultant or Agent breaches the contract then it's a breach. The addition creates an in-text contradiction concerning when information in the public domain is still protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The confidentiality obligations of this Agreem ent shall not apply to information which (a) has entered the public domain , (b) prior to disclosure hereunder was already rightfully in the receiving Party’s possession under no obligation of confidentiality , or (c) subsequent to disclosure hereunder is obtained by the receiving Party on a non-confidential basis from a third party who has the right to disclose such information to the receiving Party. However, Confidential Information disclosed to a Party’s consultant or agent, who then causes it to enter the public domain, is not exempt from confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding exceptions to confidentiality, specifically concerning information entering the public domain. The original text exempted information entering the public domain except if caused by a party's breach, which the changed text initially removes. However, the addition of 'However, Confidential Information disclosed to a Party’s consultant or agent, who then causes it to enter the public domain, is not exempt from confidentiality obligations' reintroduces an exception but only for consultants or agents, creating a direct contradiction as to when information in the public domain is still considered confidential, thus creating an in-text contradiction as the new phrase both contradicts the prior phrase from within the same block of text, and the original rule that exempted public domain information from being confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of three (3) years af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of one (1) year af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination, except customer data. Customer data confidentiality lasts only 6 months after termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a three-year confidentiality period after the business relationship ends. The modification changes this to one year but adds an exception: Confidential Information lasts indefinitely. It then adds another term, which now says, customer data confidentiality lasts for only 6 months. This adds an in-text contradiction, because if Confidential Information survives, with no end date (or any time limit to it), and now we add a Customer data term, creating contradiction with the previous statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall continue dur ing the Parties’ business relationship and bind the Parties hereto for a period of one (1) year af ter the termination of all business relationships between the Parties. Notwithstanding the foregoing, th is Agreement shall survive with respect to all Confidential Information that is di sclosed before the termination, except customer data. Customer data confidentiality lasts only 6 months after termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality. Originally, confidentiality lasted for three years after the termination of all business relationships. The changed text modifies this to one year but adds an exception: Confidential Information lasts indefinitely. It then adds another term, which now says, customer data confidentiality lasts for only 6 months. This adds an in-text contradiction, because if Confidential Information survives, with no end date (or any time limit to it), and now we add a Customer data term, creating contradiction with the previous statement, as Customer Data is Confidential Information.
